body:
As far as I can tell, [this comment](https://github.com/z3z1ma/target-bigquery/blob/9e9121a/target_bigquery/core.py#L581) is inaccurate because the `BEGIN TRANSACTION` / `COMMIT TRANSACTION` [syntax](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/transactions#transaction_scope) are not used, and they can't be used in this case either because [transactions don't support creating or dropping non-temporary tables](https://docs.cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/transactions#statements_supported_in_transactions). 

`CREATE OR REPLACE` command is an alternative which makes the table replacement atomic, making sure there's no gap in the middle where the target table is unavailable. Separating the temp table drop into a separate query ensures we only delete the temp table if the target table was successfully overwritten.

commits.messageHeadline: use CREATE OR REPLACE instead of separate DROP IF EXISTS + CREATE TABLE

title: use CREATE OR REPLACE instead of separate DROP IF EXISTS + CREATE TABLE
